entry.title: Welcome - SAP Auckland Community Events 2024
entry.content:
The local SAP Auckland Community for casual gatherings has kicked off from mid-2024. The initial SAP Inside Track event was hosted at the Lagom Auckland offices, an SAP Partner. We really appreciate those who came to the very first community event to discuss all things #SAP #BTP related.

Special thanks to our speakers Murali Shanmugham, John Paul Agcamaran and Martin Coetzee. All three shared some great insights from Sapphire, through to a recent CAP Course - complete with a demo of the new AI assistant Joule - and the importance of understanding GROW and RISE and what that means for you on your BP journey.

View the post from the 1st Auckland SAP Inside Track event:

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/activity-7211476493279846401-o4xh?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

The 2nd Auckland SAP Inside Track event is being held a day before the local Annual SAP User Group and Summit in New Zealand. This will be hosted at the Deloitte offices, another SAP Partner. 

We look forward to seeing this community grow and for the continuous variety to speakers and topics - as well as some serious socialising!

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Creation of Public Views on SAP BTP for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Introduction

With this blogpost I will describe the new possibility to create public views, which can be used by all users within your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Views

You find views in mostly every Apps in SAP Cloud ALM on the top left side.

Steps on the SAP BTP Cockpit

Open your SAP BTP cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Open the subaccount that contains your SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Choose Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Under Roles, select the role FlexPublicViewEditor and choose Add and click on Save.

 

Under Users, select all users who should be able to create public views and click on Save.

SAP Cloud ALM

It is now possible to create public views.
And every SAP Cloud ALM user can use the public views.

In the user management of SAP Cloud ALM you can now also see the new role:

That's it.

entry.title: ALM Nuggets: Erstellen öffentlicher Ansichten auf der SAP BTP für SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Einführung

In diesem Blogbeitrag beschreibe ich die neue Möglichkeit, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen, die von allen Benutzern in Ihrem SAP Cloud ALM tenant verwendet werden können.

Ansichten

Sie finden Ansichten in fast allen Apps in SAP Cloud ALM oben links.

Schritte im SAP BTP Cockpit

Öffnen Sie das SAP BTP Cockpit:
https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Öffnen Sie den Subaccount für Ihren SAP Cloud ALM tenant:
Wählen Sie: Security  > Role Collections > Create 

Name: Public View Editor
Description: Create and edit public views, which are shared with all users in your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Unter Roles, selektieren Sie die Rolle FlexPublicViewEditor und fügen diese hinzu.

Unter Users wählen Sie diejenigen Benutzer aus welche öffentliche Ansichten erstellen dürfen.

SAP Cloud ALM

Es ist jetzt möglich, öffentliche Ansichten zu erstellen.
Und jeder SAP Cloud ALM Benutzer kann die öffentlichen Ansichten verwenden.

Im User Management von SAP Cloud ALM können sie die neue Rolle nun ebenfalls sehen:

Das wars, vielen Dank fürs lesen.

entry.title: XSA Service Instances Migration from On-premise to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
In the last blog, you learned migration from an on-premise SAP HANA database to an SAP HANA Cloud database instance using the Self-Service Migration for SAP HANA Cloud. Now, will see on how to migrate the XS advanced service instances from on-premise to Cloud Foundry.

Figure: Migration Landscape

Pre-requisites

  • Connectivity Service Entitlement with the service plan “connectivity_proxy” must be enabled for your subaccount using the SAP BTP cockpit.
  • Supported for SAP HANA Database Revision 53 or greater.
  • Source and Tenant databases has XS advanced installed.
  • Migration user in the XSA tenant database of source system with necessary privileges.
  • Establish a connection to the XSA tenant database of source system using Cloud Connector.

Note: Services Instances in default SAP space is not supported for migration.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud Central, navigate to “Migrations”.
  2. Choose “Create Migration”, specify the migration information and then “Create”.
  3. In the PLAN phase,
  • Enter the virtual host and port that you specified in the cloud connector configuration.
  • Enable Cloud Connector and specify the Cloud Connector Location ID.
  • Enter the credentials of the migration user that was created in the XSA tenant database of the source system.
  • Check Compatibility with SAP HANA Cloud.

  • Tool runs a series of database and application compatibility checks. Once the Self-Service Migration tool finishes checking your database, it lists any incompatibilities found. 
  • Only checks with the severity 'CRITICAL' and 'UNKNOWN' will forbid the migration. Checks with the severity 'ERROR' or below will allow the migration; however, it is possible that some functionality may not work as expected in the target database.
  • Review and resolve the incompatibilities where possible. Choose Proceed to Preparation Phase.

    4. In the PREPARATION phase, provide the following information:

       Migrate XS advanced service instances (SAP HANA Schemas and HDI containers) to Cloud Foundry.

     As the source database has XS advanced installed and the tenant database selected for migration is enabled for XS advanced, the connection to the XS advanced database is configured through the SAP Cloud Connector.

  • Provide the login credentials of the XS advanced administrator user.
  • Configure the connection to the XS advanced runtime through Cloud Connector.
  • Choose Configure Endpoint to generate a configuration file that you  have to upload to SAP Cloud Connector in a later step.

  • Download the Configuration file and upload the same in Cloud Connector. Refer to point 9 in the link for more details.

  • Click on Save and Proceed.

  • Specify the credentials of the SAP BTP technical user.

  • Map the source and target spaces which contain the service instances to be migrated.

  • Specify the credentials of the migration user and Choose Save and Proceed to Select Target.

  • Choose the target database for the migration and enter the credentials of the DBADMIN user on the target database.

  • Confirm all the migration choices and Choose Continue to Execution Phase to proceed to the next phase of the migration.

     5. In the EXECUTION phase, choose “Start Migration” to start the migration process and monitor progress in the status bars. After the migration, a summary is displayed, and you can proceed to the VALIDATION phase.

    6. In the VALIDATION phase, the metadata and data of the migrated content are checked for consistency, also the record count of the tables to be migrated can be checked. Choose “Proceed to Finalization Phase”.

    7. In the FINALIZATION phase, the finalization process removes all artifacts that were temporarily stored by the migration service.Choose “Deactivate the migration user” and “Finalize Migration” to complete the migration and start the post-migration clean-up.

   8. The XS service instances is migrated to Cloud Foundry. A summary screen is displayed, containing information about the completed migration, including statistics about the migrated database, migration step durations, and validation statuses.

   9. “Clean Up Process” will archive the completed migration. Once the migration is archived, it serves as a reference and cannot be altered. Up to 20 migrations can be archived.

 

Hope this blog has helped! For more information refer SAP documentation SAP HANA Cloud Migration Guide.

entry.title: Joule and SAP Business AI Now available for RISE with SAP Customers
entry.content:
SAP’s vision for Business AI is to bring powerful Generative AI capabilities across all our products and services through SAP BTP to benefit the customers in achieving measurable business outcomes. As you are already aware, SAP introduced Joule in Sep 2023, a copilot that truly understands your business revolutionizing the interactions with SAP systems making every touchpoint count and every task simpler in a secure and compliant way. Joule will be embedded across SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio, delivering proactive and contextualized insights from SAP solutions and third-party sources. Joule is everywhere, was one of the most discussed topics at SAPPHIRE 2024, and needs no introduction, find out everything you need to know about Joule on this blog.

We are excited to announce Joule is now available for RISE with SAP customers with significant enhancements to RISE with SAP commercial packages focused on accelerating digital transformation and bringing the power of AI to all our customers. Joule, our AI copilot, will now be available for all existing and new RISE with SAP customers, natively integrating into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Since launching RISE with SAP in Jan 2021, our mission to transform customers into a cloud operating model had a strong momentum in RISE adoption. We have helped over 6000+ customers across various industries and geographies to transform to a cloud operating model through RISE with SAP among which 3000+ customers went live.

During this journey of cloud transformation, we have observed that it is essential for every enterprise to evolve with the latest technologies in order to adopt them and remain competitive in the market. To maintain the pace with the need for agility and innovation, while moving towards fast-changing technologies, customers need to adapt to changes that are evolving in AI technologies and innovations. As AI and Generative AI have become the top priorities for every enterprise striving to stay competitive and maintain its market position, there are a few challenges that all enterprises must acknowledge and overcome.

Below are the transformational drivers that are impacting customers to be prepared for the future.

  • Rising Customer Expectations: In the digital age, customers are more empowered than ever, driving the demand for exceptional experiences, personalized services, and seamless interactions across various channels.
  • Technological Advancements: The rapid emergence of technologies like generative AI presents both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses to stay ahead by leveraging innovation for growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Enhancing operational efficiency is crucial for enterprises aiming to streamline processes, reduce costs, and boost productivity.
  • Competitive Pressure: To maintain a competitive edge and secure market position, companies must continuously evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape.

To address these challenges, SAP Cloud ERP is infused with AI capabilities to accelerate business ability and meet business goals.

McKinsey reports that Generative AI could generate over $2.6 to $4.4 trillion incremental value annually to the global market. As AI adoption accelerates, particularly in the area of Generative AI, there is an extreme need to capitalize on this technology. Leaders across industries are prioritizing investments and closely tracking the potential impact of Generative AI, recognizing that failure to stay competitive could pose significant risks. CEOs are making these strategic decisions to avoid losing market share and maintaining their competitive edge.

 

Why only cloud? And limitations of On-Premise:

The latest technological advancements like Generative AI need high computation power for efficient and accelerated performance. It requires a level of infrastructure that can handle the speed at which the innovations are changing and, run, activate, and deploy the AI use cases effectively. Data plays a critical role in implementing AI use cases, with the volume of data growing rapidly each day. To efficiently process and fine-tune this data for optimal and accelerated performance, the latest and most powerful infrastructure that can scale according to evolving requirements is essential.

The on-premise environments become challenging when scaling the systems to meet increased AI demand. The computation power for on-premise systems becomes costly and time-consuming. Significant resources and infrastructure are required to grow and maintain hardware and software systems. The on-premise environment presents significant challenges, making a cloud a more effective option considering scalability, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

Benefits to Customers 

By adopting AI, customers can gain benefits that can impact both their Business and People. In the field of business, AI enhances decision-making faster, with data-driven insights and recommendations, ensuring safer and more informed choices. For individuals, AI transforms work through automation and streamlines user experiences, ultimately driving better business outcomes

Benefits of AI for Business:

Reinvent Customer Service with personalized experiences that address customer needs quickly.

Boost Finance Excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.

Drive Operational Excellence by optimizing manufacturing and asset performance and elevating product quality.

Advance Intelligent Order Fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

Benefits of AI for People:

Empower End Users to increase efficiency and eliminate redundant tasks ​

Interact with Business using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.​

Uncover Insights that would otherwise be challenging or time-consuming to discover 

 

SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP

Aligning with SAP’s Business AI strategy, SAP launched the integration of Joule starting with Success Factors in Dec 2023. Now, Joule is available for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud for RISE with SAP customers. Joule offers a navigational pattern helping in navigating to relevant SAP Fiori apps and letting the user work directly in the app’s web UI for new and occasional users without prior knowledge of individual SAP Fiori apps.

RISE customers will have the advantage of experiencing generative AI capabilities across SAP cloud ERP. All existing and new RISE customers will have access to Joule to quickly access apps, effectively gain insights on business objects, and speedily resolve user issues. There are a few prerequisites to access Joule on SAP S/4HANA quoted below:

Prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA (requires administrator role):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or later.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, with the following entitlements
    • Joule Entitlements - Joule(das-application), please refer to license topic
    • SAP Workzone, standard edition
    • SAP Cloud Connectivity
    • Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Cloud Connector with Principal Propagation
    • System Mapping to expose the required HTTP protocol

To know more about the capabilities of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition and complete the configuration guide follow this link.

Commercial Packages

As part of RISE with SAP offerings, entitlements are provided with different customer profile packages like Base Edition, Premium Edition, and Premium Plus Edition. Joule is accessible in all available packages Base, Premium, and Premium Plus for RISE customers. Joule will be accessible by all existing and new RISE customers fulfilling the prerequisites mentioned above to use capabilities. Joule and Generative AI will be part of the new SKU as entitlements under the Artificial Intelligence domain.

RISE Base Edition: Provides small organizations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition functionalities with a lean setup

RISE Premium Edition: Enables organization’s business transformation supported by built-in AI capabilities into SAP applications, process automation, low-code business application development, and access to supplier networks.

RISE Premium Plus Edition: Extends premium by unlocking more AI scenarios, including sustainability solutions, and advanced finance capabilities.

AI has significant potential to accelerate business processes and optimize decision-making. Since AI is at the top of every enterprise’s priority list, we are constantly innovating to deliver more AI-powered business processes and bringing the capabilities of Business AI as an entitlement as part of the SKU for RISE with SAP. Generative AI capabilities are available for RISE Premium and RISE Premium Plus packages where respective AI units are available based on the package. RISE with SAP Premium customers are provided with AI units to pilot AI-driven innovations like SAP Cash Application, SAP Business Integrity Screening, and SAP Service Management.

SAP AI Unit is a single SKU. SAP’s AI use cases are commercialized as capabilities within a single SKU regardless of LoBs. All customers can license SAP AI Unit to purchase AI units but to be able to use specific AI capabilities, you need to fulfill the licensing pre-requisites.

AI units are like a virtual currency that allows you to consume a wide set of AI use cases across different LoB solutions within SAP which included Joule overages. AI units are based on annual subscriptions and are sold in a block of 100 AI units. The minimum block to purchase is 1 per year and must be used by the end of the year as all unused AI units will be lost if not consumed by the end of the year.

Commercials & Estimator

RISE customers will have entitlements of 2500 Joule messages per FUE per contract year free of charge to interact with SAP A/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Once the entitled number of messages is consumed and you are willing to continue accessing Joule, you can purchase additional messages with AI units SKU. A message is an electronic communication exchanged via the capability of cloud service. For cloud services like Joule, a user request, together with the corresponding response from Joule is considered as 1 message.

Customers can use the AI Unit Estimator to estimate x number of AI units required to run the selected AI capabilities and can request a quote from the estimator directly. After selecting the AI feature, the customer can add the number of transactions (in a block of 10,000) you would need for the selected AI feature. Cost estimation is automatically shown based on the selection and a quote can be created from the same page for the selected AI feature and the transactions based on the requirement.

Another way where customers can access the AI services is from the SAP for Me dashboard under the Portfolio & Products tab by selecting Business AI and activating the AI feature. The features are listed based on the SAP products they belong to, where customers can select whether to activate the feature or not. The customer has the option to view the consumption for the respective feature or calculate cost estimation for the feature by selecting an estimator for the respective feature.

Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP offers innovation adopter services to help you jump-start your projects with SAP’s latest products and solutions. SAP Early Adopter Care connects customers with products to help them simplify, innovate, and digitize fast. Through the support of the Early Adopter Care project coach, we enable two-way communication, and transparency, and allow stakeholders to minimize project risk.

Benefits of the EAC program:

Support with early implementation of SAP’s latest innovations.

Direct channel to development for product and solution insights.

To join the Early adopter care program for Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition, check out this link. Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition started on 15th July and is open till 31st Dec 2024.

Conditions for participation:

Participation in the early adopter care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following requisites.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01
  • Plans to use Joule in production
  • Willingness to become a reference
  • The full set of restrictions can be found here General Constraints for Joule & Constraints for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Customer Engagement Initiative: AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

What is in for you?

  • Opportunity to define and shape the product's future.
  • Gain early insights and actively engage to learn and shape the new innovations that matter.
  • Access and engage directly with the product engineering teams.

How to get engaged with us?

Visit this link and register your company for this opportunity.

 

Joule Integration – Step-by-step guide

To configure Joule and integrate with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, check the below blogs for a step-by-step guide.

Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Unifying Your Business Operations: Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) need more than just robust ERP systems to stay competitive. they need integrated digital workspaces that unify access to all their critical applications. SAP Build Work Zone, when integrated with SAP Business One, offers a powerful solution to streamline operations, enhance collaboration, and boost productivity. This blog post provides an overview of SAP Build Work Zone and its use cases for SAP Business customers, as well as the overview of this integration, concluding with recommended e-learning resources for those interested in exploring the details further.

 

SAP Build Work Zone Overview

SAP Build Work Zone is a digital workplace solution that offers a unified, role-based user experience, integrating applications, content, and processes in a single interface. It comes in two editions:

 

SAP Build Work Zone Use cases

For SAP Business One customers, where often only about 10% of employees use the ERP system, there's an opportunity for partners to extend their reach by targeting the non-ERP users and even non-Employee users with Work Zone solutions. Traditionally, these users have relied on third-party or custom-built solutions. In addition, SAP Work Zone Standard Edtion can complement other BTP apps and services, particularly since it is included at no additional cost with Build Apps and Build Process Automation.

The Advanced Edition is also a cost-effective option for replacing third-party groupware or intranet/extranet solutions.

This approach enables partners to generate recurring cloud revenue and additional income from contents and services.

 

Integration with SAP Business One

This illustrates the Build Work Zone demo site, featuring tiles and cards.

  • HR Space includes the Employee Self-Service page with two tile apps:
    • Employee Lookup app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Employee Store Apps - 3rd party UI5 app
  • Sales Space contains two pages:
    • Overview Page features ...
      • Company dashboard - brought from SAP Analytics Cloud Story
      • Business Partners app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Sales Processing Page is divided into two sections “Sales Orders” and “Invoicing”, each hosting ...
      • Business One Web Client URL app - opens SAP Business One Web Client Views
      • UI Integration card - built with SAP Business Application Studio with no-code manner. it displays the most-concise pieces of business data and enable straightforward and decisive user action in a limited-space container, helping users structure their work in an intuitive and dynamic way.

 

This site was configured with the view mode "Space and Pages – New Experience" to host UI integration cards. 

 

Then how to create a tile and card in a site? Here are three ways to manage business contents in a site.

 

Overview of Step-by-Step Process to create the above demo site in Build Work Zone standard edition, integrated with SAP Business One

Prerequisites

  1. Maintain a destination to represent SAP Business One service layer in BTP subaccount.
  2. Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (IAS) to let IAS users work as business users across the different systems with single sign-on. 
  3. Optionally create applications in the SAP IAS admin console for SAP Business One if you want to achieve single sign-on with SAP Business One Web Client.

Configuration steps

  1. Create the Work Zone standard edition in BTP subaccount.
  2. Prepare for apps for Tiles and Cards for the above demo site.
    • Create applications in Build Apps and/or Business Application Studio and deploy them to HTML5 repository (the managed router created by Build Work Zone) in the BTP subaccount.
    • Create UI integration cards and build a content package for UI integration cards in Business Application Studio.
    • Create a SAP Analytics Cloud Story.
  3. Create a new site in the Build Work Zone Site Manager
  4. Create the 1st space for HR 
  5. Create the 2nd space for sales and its 1st page
  6. Deploy the content package for the UI integration cards to Work Zone and assign the role
  7. Create the 2nd page for the sales space, including UI integration cards

 

Conclusion and Call-to-action

Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One provides a powerful solution for SMEs looking to unify their IT environments, enhance productivity, and improve business agility. With its flexible capabilities, role-based access, and mobile readiness, SAP Build Work Zone is an essential tool for businesses aiming to streamline their operations and empower their employees and business partners.

For the detailed step-by-step guides on how to implement this integration and explore its full potential, access the e-learning course on the SAP PartnerEdge portal (S-user required) : 

  • Presentation
  • Step-by-step video
  • How to create UI Integration Cards exercise

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Generative AI with SAP, which is focused on understanding how SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across its portfolio.

In this series we are learning:

  • How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio.
  • What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it?
  • What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc.
  • What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy?
  • Hands-on, Real-life examples and many more.

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

  • Part 1 – Introduction to SAP Business AI 
  • Part 2 – Components of SAP Business AI 
  • Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot 
  • Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP [Current blog]
  • Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core
  • Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad
  • Part 7 – Get Started with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  • part 8 - Hello World using SAP AI Core
  • Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub
  • Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine

 

This is the 4th blog in the series, where will learn about AI Foundation.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Let’s first quickly revise what SAP BTP is

In January 2021, SAP introduced a unique and powerful solution offering called SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). SAP BTP is the technical foundation of entire SAP ecosystem and plays a crucial role for all SAP customers and partners.

In other word, SAP BTP is a portfolio of SAP solutions and services that are brought under one umbrella. These services, and solutions helps organizations build a new cloud solution or extend SAP systems.

For more information on SAP BTP, see Explaining SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to a Beginner

 

AI Foundation

Imagine you are developers/architect working for an SAP customer or partner.

  • You have been assigned to build a new application on SAP BTP or extend an existing SAP solution.
  • You need to incorporate AI capabilities into your application/extension.
  • To do so, you are looking for ready-to-use AI services and tools.
  • You want to make it simple and avoid individual contracts with 3rd party AI vendors and LLM providers.

AI foundation is an offering from SAP, which fulfil all these requirements.

 

So, what exactly is AI Foundation?

AI foundation on SAP BTP is a set of ready-to-use AI services and tools, which:

  • enable customers and partners (and SAP’s own development teams as well)
  • to create AI-powered extensions and applications
  • in a reliable, secure, and scalable way

 

In other words, AI Foundation is the set of AI services and tools on SAP BTP for developers to incorporate AI capabilities into applications/extensions.

Below image gives a high-level picture of AI foundation.

 

AI Foundation on SAP BTP provides an abstraction for SAP Customers and Partners

SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers to ensure that SAP customers are able to reap the benefit of AI innovations without going into individual contract with 3rd party vendors.

For example, if you need to use GPT LLM, you do not need to buy any separate license, you can access it via Generative AI Hub on SAP BTP. From Generative AI Hub, you can access these large language models from multiple vendors easily.

 

Components of AI Foundation

Below image, shows the major components of AI Foundation.

 

 

Let’s look into each of these components in detail.

 

1. AI services

AI services (aka AI Business Services) are ready-to-use, reusable AI services on SAP BTP.

  • Developers can use these AI services add AI capabilities into their application
  • These services include AI models pre-trained on business-relevant data
  • It covers wide range of business use cases.

 

These AI services are broadly categorized in 3 buckets as shown in below image.

 

Document Processing

AI foundation provides a ready-to-use service Document Information Extraction for intelligent document processing.

The Document Information Extraction service:

  • uses machine learning to automate information extraction processes from a document
  • helps to process large amounts of business documents that have content in headers and tables.

With Document Information Extraction service, we can:

  • Process more documents efficiently with fewer errors and difficulties
  • Increase quality and compliance mechanisms

We can use the extracted information to automate business processes. For example, automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes while making sure that invoices and payables match.

 

Recommendation

There are 3 services under this bucket to enhance human-decision making:

  • Personalized Recommendation
  • Data Attribute Recommendation
  • Business Entity Recognition

 

Personalized Recommendation is a generic reusable service, which:

  • utilizes machine learning to provide customers personalized recommendations based on their browsing history and/or item description.
  • We can train and use machine learning models to deliver these recommendations across a wide range of business scenarios.

For example, SAP is leveraging this service to process more than 4.2 million learning recommendations for SAP SuccessFactors customers each month.

 

Data Attribute Recommendation service can be used if there are missing values in the dataset. The service:

  • uses free text, numbers and categories as input
  • to classify entities such as products, stores and users into multiple classes
  • and to predict the value of missing numerical attributes in your data records.

With Data Attribute Recommendation we can:

  • Automate and speed up data management processes
  • Reduce errors and manual efforts in data maintenance
  • Increase data consistency and accuracy

 

Business Entity Recognition is a service which helps to detect and highlight any type of business entity in unstructured text. For example, this service can be used to automatically extract the context from incoming emails with invoice inquiries, automating recurring tasks associated with answering queries about the status and payment of invoices.

 

Machine Translation

SAP is being used all over the world and in almost all the languages and that brings an important aspect of Translation of Texts to all the required languages. To accelerate and automate the translation process, SAP offers SAP Translation Hub service under AI Foundation.

SAP Translation Hub is a reusable service to translate content into multiple languages. This service helps you speed up the translation of software texts (e.g. user interfaces) and related documents – with high quality and accuracy. You can use a repository of SAP-approved translations and terminology as well as machine translation, which has been trained with focus on SAP-specific content.

Note: You can find the list of all the AI services and their details in SAP Discovery Center or you can visit the official product documentation.

 

How can SAP Customers/Partners use AI Services?

Customers and partner can integrate the SAP AI Services into their own processes and applications by connecting and calling the provided API endpoints accordingly.

Let’s understand this with an example.

 

Use Personalized Recommendation service to give visitors to a website personalized recommendation

Let’s say we want to use Personalized Recommendation service to give visitors to your website highly personalized recommendations based on their browsing history and/or item description.

Below diagram shows the high-level architecture diagram for this use-case.

 

We can achieve this as a sequence of steps shown below.

Note: You can follow this tutorial to get step-by-step guide for this entire process.

 

Similar to the approach we took for Personalized Recommendation, we can use other AI services as shown in below diagram.

 

Few more tutorials on SAP AI services

Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Business Documents and Enrich Data

Use Pre-Trained Machine Learning Models to Process Unstructured Text

Shape Machine Learning to Process Custom Business Documents

Use Machine Learning to Classify Data Records

Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents

 

2. Generative AI Management

Second major component of AI Foundation is Generative AI Management. Under this bucket, AI foundation provides an offering called Generative AI Hub – which is an BTP service to explore different LLM models.

Let’s have a close look into Generative AI Hub.

 

What is Generative AI Hub?

 

 

Generative AI Hub helps us to fast-track development of any generative AI apps on SAP BTP. It enables developers to instantly access broad range of large language models (LLMs) from different providers, such as GPT-4 by Azure OpenAI or Open-Source Falcon 40B.

Generative AI – an BTP service to explore different LLM models.

 

We can use Generative AI Hub to orchestrate multiple generative AI models:

  • Either programmatically via SAP AI Core
  • or via the playground within SAP AI Launchpad

 

The generative AI hub also provides tooling for prompt engineering, experimentation, and other capabilities to speed-up the development of generative AI applications on BTP, in a secure and trusted way.

 

Below image shows a quick glimpse of how we can submit a prompt to multiple LLMs and compare the generated outcomes to identify the best-suited model using Generative AI Hub.

Disclaimer: The above image is taken from this blog

Note: For more information on Generative AI Hub, refer to Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub [to be published]

 

AI Workload Management

AI workload management are targeted for developers who wants to build a new AI model from scratch or run an AI workflow or execute an AI asset in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. AI workload management offers all the necessary tools to create AI models, train them, evaluate their accuracy and publish them for inferencing.

Under AI Workload Management bucket, AI foundation provides two services:

  • SAP AI Core
  • SAP AI Launchpad.

 

What is SAP AI Core?

SAP AI Core is a service available on SAP BTP, which offers a powerful AI Runtime. We can use SAP AI Core to train and deploy our AI models cost-efficiently at scale while preserving privacy and compliance.

One of the main benefits of SAP AI Core is that we can choose underlying infrastructure (GPUs, CPU Cores, Memory) from a broad range of plans, as well as avail built-in autoscaling. You can have a quick look on the various resource plan available for SAP AI Core here.

Note: For more information on SAP AI Core, refer to Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core [to be published]

 

What is SAP AI Launchpad?

SAP AI Launchpad is a service on SAP BTP, which can be used to manage and run AI workflows and AI models across multiple AI runtimes (including SAP AI Core).

SAP AI Launchpad provides generative AI capabilities via the Generative AI Hub.

SAP AI launchpad also provides centralize AI lifecycle management for our AI scenarios with a convenient user interface. We can monitor model performance statistics continuously and retrain as needed.

Note: For more information on SAP AI Launchpad, refer to Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI Launchpad [to be published]

 

Few Important point regarding SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

  • SAP AI Core is natively integrated with SAP AI Launchpad.
  • The SAP AI Launchpad offers an easy-to-use interface to manage AI workflow administration, processes, and tasks.
  • SAP AI Core can also be accessed by other supported tools as well, such as Postman or programmatically using Python.
  • Technically, SAP AI Launchpad is a multitenant software as a service (SaaS) application on SAP BTP.
  • AI Launchpad can be used to manage multiple AI runtime (e.g., multiple AI Core instances).

 

Business Data and Context

The most important differentiator of SAP system from other AI providers is that – SAP systems contains huge business data of customers. These business data is the key to leverage AI models to solve real business problems.

With solutions like SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Datasphere, SAP helps providing business data and context to the AI models. SAP HANA Cloud vector engine has specifically been designed for generative AI use-case.

 

What is SAP HANA Cloud vector engine?

The vector engine is a new addition to SAP HANA Cloud, a database-as-a-service delivered on SAP BTP. Using SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, we can ground AI with unique business data.

The SAP HANA Cloud vector engine supports efficient storage and processing of vector data. This means we can execute joins, apply filters, and perform selects by combining vector data with various data types, including transactional, spatial, graph, and JSON data, all within the same SQL environment.

 

Here are some key benefits of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine:

  • Simplify the data architecture, management, and security with the storage of vectors and other enterprise data within the same database.
  • Interact with all types of data (including vectors) using SQL.
  • Gain new insights into data by combining spatial, graph, JSON, and custom SQLScript with vector-based queries.
  • Incorporate vector use cases in solutions with the SAP HANA Cloud clients (including Python), the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA (hana-ml), and CAP.

Note: For more information on SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, refer to Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine [to be published]

 

SAP HANA Cloud also provide AI function libraries - Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and Automated Predictive Library (APL). These libraries allow us to implement classification, regression or time series forecasting scenarios applied directly to the business data.

SAP also provides a solution, SAP Datasphere for data management. SAP Datasphere includes AI in several ways, for example in data integration, data quality & cleansing (such as correcting missing values or duplicates), data governance, analytics (such as detecting trends, analyze historical data, and make predictions), and natural language processing.

 

Foundation Model

Under AI Foundation umbrella, SAP BTP provides a wide range of foundation models that can be used to implement various generative AI use-cases. To provide a seamless experience to SAP customers, SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and LLMs providers, such as Google, Meta, IBM, Nvidia etc.

These foundation models, help ensure that SAP customers keep up with the fast pace of innovation and have the flexibility of choosing right model for their scenario.

 

What exactly is a foundation model?

A foundation model is a deep learning model which is trained on huge amount of data, usually with unsupervised learning. The model can be adapted to perform a wide range of tasks such as text generation, image generation, video generation, sentiment analysis, information extract etc.

Foundation models can be considered as general-purpose technologies that can support a diverse range of use cases. Since these models are pre-trained on vast datasets using powerful hardware and techniques, they can be used to save time and computational costs compared to training new models for each specific application.

Below image summarizes important points about foundation model.

To know more about foundation model, you may refer to What is Foundation Model?

 

SAP Foundation Models - SAP's own foundation model to redefine enterprise AI

Let’s first understand why customers need a foundation model from SAP.

Why general-purpose foundation models may not be enough for SAP customers?

There are many general-purpose foundation models available which are pre-trained on huge data. Under AI Foundation, SAP provides easy access to many of these models such as GPT-4, Llama2, Falcon-40b, Claude2 and many more. While these models offer amazing opportunities, they also have limitations. For example, LLMs may rely on outdated training data and lack company-specific data and business process context.

As an example, imagine LLM as a colleague who is very smart and knowledgeable. This colleague can answer most of your queries. However, he may not know what has happened in the world last year and he also does not have any idea about your company’s internal process and policies.

For example, let’s say you ask a LLM - “What do you think about the offer from our most important supplier last week?”.

An LLM can only work with the initial training data and may nor provide accurate answer.

However, if you provide enough business data and context to the LLM, then you can get the answer you expect.

 

Supplementing this lack of information is where SAP Foundation Model comes into the picture. SAP is trying to adapt the LLMs with the SAP business data and context to create it’s own models which will be best suited for customers.

 

So, what exactly is SAP foundation model?

SAP is designing its own foundation models by combining general-purpose large language models with its deep understanding of business processes and business data. These models will help customers to get context-specific response.

The SAP foundation model will be very useful in all core business areas such as finance, sales, supply chain, etc. It will be able to address questions we face every day in business that large language models cannot answer properly, such as predicting invoice payment dates and supplier delivery quality or proposing efficiency improvements to a business process.

SAP foundation model will also allow for fine-tuning for different scenarios, will further unlock and strengthen SAP’s leading position in embedded AI. Customers will benefit from high-performance models trained on rich SAP data, achieving better predictive results.

 

Please note that customers don’t have to wait for these SAP foundation models to be ready before they can leverage context from business data. There is another approach called Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which also can be used to provide business data context and give customers better results right away.

Note: For more information on RAG, refer to Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine [to be published]

 

What’s Next?

Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core [To be published soon]

Disclaimer– All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: Document Information Extraction
entry.content:
Methodology

SAP BTP uses OCR (Optical Character Recognition) capabilities that enable the conversion of scanned documents or images containing text into editable and searchable data. By leveraging OCR, businesses can extract text from various documents, including invoices, receipts, contracts, and more, thus automating data entry processes and reducing manual errors.

To Start, go to SAP BTP Cockpit Trial -> Services MarketPlace ->Document Information Extraction Trial -> Create Instance

Step 1 — Create Instance

Service — Document Information Extraction Trial

Plan — blocks_of_100

Runtime Environment — Cloud Foundry

Space — dev/trial

Instance Name — Food Receipts/ Any relevant name

 

Step 2— Create Schema

  • Write Name according to the problem statement (In this case Food_Receipt_Invoice)
  • Document Type is Custom & OCR is used for type Document or Scene Text based on scenario

 

Step 3— Add fields to the Schema

Mention Field Names as per the Information you need to extract.Mention the Data Type as per field.Setup Type can be Manual or Auto.In this case we use auto.

 

Step 4 — Activate Schema

 

Step 5 — Upload the necessary Document

  • Select document type as Custom as you can select any document you want.

Schema should be Food Receipt Schema (One you created just now).

Click on confirm & go to Documents.

Step 6 — Review the extraction results

 

You can also review the extraction results in csv formats with more detailed information about the document.

Leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform for document information extraction empowers organizations to unlock the value hidden within their unstructured data. By employing methodologies such as OCR, NLP, ML models, and integration with intelligent technologies, businesses can automate document processing tasks, extract actionable insights, and drive informed decision-making. Whether it’s enhancing operational efficiency, improving compliance, or enhancing customer experiences, SAP BTP provides a comprehensive platform for organizations to harness the power of document information extraction in today’s digital era.

Step by Step Guide to the process - 

https://medium.com/@mjoshi2669/document-information-extraction-using-sap-btp-cockpit-1d9886b39096

entry.title: An AI Use Case in Energy Procurement with SAP HANA Cloud vector engine and SAP generative AI hub
entry.content:
Introduction:

Since the release of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, numerous internal and external stakeholders have been using it productively to achieve their AI business goals. In this blog, I would like to share the details of one such business use case developed by Wipro and certified as a Validated Partner Use Case by SAP. Wipro worked closely with the teams from SAP Hack2Build and SAP BTP Gen AI Lighthouse Program streams to bring their vision to action, specifically in the domain of energy procurement. These innovation-boosting programs from SAP highlight modern use cases leveraging SAP's technology stack, including the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine and SAP generative AI hub within the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

Use Case: Smart Energy Procurement Advisor (SEPA)

The Problem Statement:

The business scenario centers around the challenges faced by energy procurement executives due to sudden surges in energy demand, that require quick identification of optimized procurement strategies. The complexity is also increased by the multitude of procurement options, including renewable energy, long-term contracts, spot markets, and demand-side response (DSR), as well as unstructured contract terms and fluctuating market prices. These factors make the procurement process tedious and complex, with a high dependency on experienced executives. Additionally, there is a lack of visibility and clarity in the rationale behind procurement decisions, making it difficult for the broader organization to understand and participate in the process.

Additionally, the early stages of implementing AI solutions at Wipro presented several challenges, particularly in maintaining security and data integrity. Ensuring that business processes and proprietary practices remained protected while integrating advanced technologies was crucial. Addressing these security challenges was essential for Wipro to gain customer trust and drive the adoption of AI-driven solutions.

Solution:

Wipro’s Smart Energy Procurement Advisor (SEPA) is a Gen AI-powered platform specifically designed to address the complexities and challenges faced by energy procurement executives. By leveraging Natural Language Query (NLQ) capabilities and the Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) model, SEPA efficiently navigates the vast array of procurement options. The platform employs multi-dimensional spatial distance ranking to deliver the most relevant historical results, supports the simulation of custom scenarios, and facilitates stakeholder approvals with comprehensive track and trace features. Additionally, SEPA includes a dashboard for event management and detailed closure tracking. With future integration plans for Joule, SEPA enhances the procurement process by reducing costs, improving demand fulfillment, ensuring compliance, decreasing reliance on experienced executives, and strengthening tracking, governance, and auditability.

Building on the capabilities of SEPA, the introduction of the SAP Generative AI Hub and the enhancement of the SAP HANA Cloud database with the vector engine within the SAP BTP landscape provided a robust and secure foundation for Wipro's AI initiatives. This setup allowed Wipro to access Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-3.5 and GPT-4.0 within a controlled and secure environment, ensuring that all data processing remained within the BTP suite, thereby mitigating the risks of data leakage or unauthorized access. The integration of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine further enabled the efficient handling and storage of vector data, crucial for developing industry-grade AI solutions. Together, these tools empowered Wipro to leverage advanced AI capabilities while maintaining strict security protocols, offering a secure, efficient, and scalable platform for their AI-driven innovations.

Use Case Architecture

 

Value Add of SAP HANA Cloud vector engine in realizing Wipro's use case to full potential

Wipro has been building AI solutions for long and more recently with GenAI. With flexible access to LLMs through the SAP Generative AI Hub, building powerful solutions has become relatively easy. Wipro’s SEPA leverages frameworks like Langchain, Text-ada-002 for embedding, and the SAP AI core (SAP Generative AI Hub) for accessing LLMs. The result is a robust Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based solution, seamlessly powered by  the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine.

As you might see from the solution architecture, SEPA simplifies data extraction from the S/4 stack into the SAP BTP landscape, where it becomes consumable by the Gen AI workflow. This allows for inclusion of S/4 data using natural language queries. Additionally, developers utilized SAP Build Code, which supports Joule for GenAI assisted coding.

In essence, Wipro's SEPA solution is a great example of the value added by the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine. The key advantages for this use case includes:

  1. Fast Vector Search: The vector engine enables quick retrieval of similar vectors, enhancing the efficiency of data processing and response generation.
  2. Inherent scalability, high performance, and efficient storage – The usual strong points of SAP HANA Cloud are bolstered now with vector support.
  3. Cost Efficiency Upgrading to SAP HANA Cloud incorporates vector support without extra charges, making it a cost-effective solution for enterprises.
  4. Integrated Data Management: Integrated with structured cross-sectional data, it seamlessly combines vector fields with traditional fields, supporting similar structures and enabling faster development.

Stay tuned for more Partner Use Case Highlights

The successful implementation of the SEPA solution showcases the potential of the SAP Hack2Build & SAP BTP Gen AI Lighthouse program in driving innovation and solving complex industry challenges. Please make sure to check this solution out on our SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Stay tuned for more blogs explaining additional use cases that have benefited from SAP’s tech stack. These upcoming posts will provide deeper insights into various AI-driven solutions, their development processes, and their transformative impacts on different sectors. Join us on this journey as we continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with AI and SAP's advanced technologies.

Note: This blog is a collaborative effort between SAP HANA Cloud Product Management and Wipro, highlighting the innovative use case enabled by the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine. For those interested in implementation details of this solution, Prasanna Mahalingam (prasanna.mahalingam1@wipro.com) from Wipro is available as the primary contact.

References:

  1. Embedding Business Context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine
  2. GenAI Reference Solution Architecture on SAP Business Technology Platform
  3. SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine: Quick FAQ Reference
  4. Try for yourself:
    • Harnessing Generative AI Capabilities with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
    • Explore SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine via Basic Trial

entry.title: Terraform Provider for SAP BTP version 1.6.0 - what’s new
entry.content:
Here we go again – welcome 1.6.0

Another month has passed, and I hope you had a great summer vacation. And as another month has passed, say welcome to the August release aka version 1.6.0 of the Terraform provider for SAP BTP is out. Let’s look at what has been added since the last release.

Of course, you also find all the information in the release notes in the GitHub repository:

Feature: Add timeout block for subaccount subscription

Up to now you had the option to configure timeouts for environments and service instances to avoid running into timeouts during the execution of your Terraform script.

We got the feedback that this is also needed for the resources of subscriptions. So here you go. With this release you can also define a timeout block when defining subscriptions in Terraform.

If you are already using this resource type, no worries, the change is backward compatible, so no changes needed in your existing configurations. They will behave the same as in the prior releases.

Feature: Add dashboard URL to service instance

Due to community feedback, we have been made aware that the field “dashboard_url” is missing as read-only attribute and this would be quite handy to have this. Well, with release 1.6.0 you have now also access to this field when creating the resource.

The documentation was updated accordingly: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_service_instance

Feature: Add retry logic for rate limit for entitlement

Some of our users reported that they ran into issues when setting up entitlements for bigger landscapes in CI/CD pipelines, so not only a lot of entitlements but also an execution in parallel. Due to many calls to the platform API triggered via the provider, the system ran into a rate limit error.

This error was propagated and consequently the execution of the Terraform run was aborted.

With release 1.6.0 we enhanced the implementation for entitlements to react a bit more sophisticated when an according error is raised by the underlying API. In detail the processing will not be aborted, but a retry loop is triggered. This should mitigate the issue of running into a rate limit-

Feature: Resource to manage XSUAA API Credentials

As you might know the Terraform provider for SAP BTP stands on the shoulder of the giant BTP CLI (BTP CLI server to be precise). The BTP CLI commands evolve and consequently we must then do the necessary adoptions on the Terraform side to get the new commands in as resources.

This release contains the results of the exercise for the BTP CLI command “security/api-credential” (https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/btp-cli-command-reference/security-api-credential). Version 1.6.0 contains the corresponding resources. You find all the details in the documentation:

  • Global Account: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/globalaccount_api_credential
  • Directory: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/directory_api_credential
  • Subaccount: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest/docs/resources/subaccount_api_credential

Fix: data source subaccount - empty placeholders in error message

Due to a glitch in the data source subaccount the error message template that was raised due to a wrong configuration was empty and one might say not a very big help for you to pin down the error in your Terraform configuration.

With release 1.6.0 the error template gets filled correctly and the error message now helps you in pinning down the issue as you would expect it.

How to update?

I guess you already know the drill, but anyhow. The update of the provider version is straightforward. Depending on your configuration in the provider, you must adjust the version constraint to 1.6.0 and execute a “terraform init -upgrade”.

That’s it.

You find the documentation of the enhanced resources in the Terraform registry: https://registry.terraform.io/providers/SAP/btp/latest

Feedback, questions, bugs, feature requests

The procedure hasn’t change: if you want to interact with us, use the GitHub repository https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp:

  • To file a bug, please open an issue in the repository.
  • To file a feature request, please file it in the repository.
  • If you have a question, want to discuss ideas, or do some “show & tell”, feel free to start a GitHub Discussion.

There are also some feature requests open that you can vote for. Check for the issues that are label as “enhancement” and “pending decision“ via this link https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Aenhancement+label%3Apending-decision.

What else?

You are certainly aware that we are also working on a Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry that you find here https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-cloudfoundry. The provider has already a quite mature state and the first release candidate is out. A Expect to see an update (a GA version???) around TechEd (I guess they call it “event-driven delivery” ;-)).  

We are also working on the functionality to make your life easier when it comes to do the Terraform import for landscapes (working Title Terraform Exporter) as described in this issue https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/issues/724. We are making very good progress, so I hope to share some news soon.  

What is next?

Do you want a sneak peek at what is coming next and when we plan to deliver the next release? You find that information in the milestones on our GitHub repository https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/milestones.

Be aware that plans and dates might change through the course of development.

With that … happy Terraforming!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition – the Way Ahead
entry.content:
Today, SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition is available in the Cloud Platform, enterprise agreement environment (CPEA).   

How is SAP Analytics Cloud, public edition different from SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition? 

The current service for SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition has limited BI functionality and is priced per tenant.  It is limited to one HANA Cloud data source and 150 simultaneous users.  The full-use services for SAP Analytics Cloud, public edition will include full BI and Planning functionality and be priced on a user metric, just as the subscription version of SAP Analytics Cloud.  The same minimums will be maintained between CPEA(Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) and subscription.  Functionally, it will be the exact same as SAP Analytics Cloud public edition sold as subscription.   

 What is the Process for Technical Migration? 

 At any time, a customer can start the new full-use service and use Analytics Content Network to migrate their content from SAC, embedded edition tenants to CPEA SAC, public edition tenants.   You will need to start the SAC, public edition service first, in order to import the content. 

 What Happens to the Existing Customers? 

 During your CPEA contract duration, you’re still able to access the SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition in the SAP BTP cockpit. After your contract ends, the SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition instance will be removed from your sub-account and you won’t be able to provision new service instances. Additionally, you won't be able to access your existing applications, create new ones, nor use the export functionality.

entry.title: Announcing BTP Trial Open Hours - Connect with the Product Team!
entry.content:
If you’re exploring the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Trial, we’ve just released something you won’t want to miss – the BTP Trial Open Hours. It’s your chance to connect directly with the trial product team, share your feedback, and ask those burning questions.

What’s BTP Trial Open Hours All About?

It’s simple: You are using the BTP Trial account, and we want to learn about your experiences. During these 1-hour sessions, you get to chat directly with 1-2 trial product team members. It’s an open forum to share what you love about the trial, what could be improved, and where we can do better - all anonymously, of course.

But it’s not just about sharing your thoughts. Got questions? We’ll do our best to help you navigate the platform and make the most of your trial.

Here’s How You Sign Up

To participate in BTP Trial Open Hours, you must have an active BTP Trial account. If not, follow this tutorial to get started. Then, use this direct link to book your session, or alternatively you can access it through the BTP Trial Cockpit:

  1. Log into the BTP Trial Cockpit.
  2. Click "Give Feedback": You’ll see this option in the top right corner. Click on it to get started.
  3. Sign up for BTP Trial Open Hours.

See you around!

Emre

entry.title: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Invoicing with KSeF Using SAP Event Mesh and CAP
entry.content:
This blog post aims to propose an example on how SAP Business Events can be used for integrating SAP S/4HANA systems with KSeF.

The National System of e-Invoices (KSeF) is a new document exchange platform introduced in Poland. All invoices, including those generated in SAP systems, must be registered in KSeF starting from February 2026.

Architecture overview

The solution architecture relies on SAP Business Events and the Cloud Application Programming Model. When an invoice is created, a business event is triggered and handled by a CAP application. The necessary invoice data is then retrieved through an OData API. Once all the required information is gathered, the invoice is sent to KSeF via the KSeF API.

Billing Document Events enabling

Enterprise Event Enablement is a technical component in SAP S/4HANA that facilitates event-driven communication across different platforms. It enables seamless event exchange by providing a wide range of standard business events (a comprehensive list of these events can be found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub).

In this context, we are particularly interested in Billing Documents Events. Whenever a Billing Document is created, changed, or deleted, a standard event is triggered. To consume this event in our CAP application, we need to configure an event channel to our SAP Event Mesh instance (detailed guides are available for both Cloud and OnPremise configurations). Once the configuration is complete, you can consume events in your SAP Event Mesh instance.

Billing Document Created event consumed in SAP Event Mesh

Building a CAP application for event consumption 

The next step is to build a CAP application in the SAP BTP environment that will consume events raised by your S/4HANA system. We are leveraging the Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) because it integrates well with SAP Advanced Messaging. A detailed guide on how to consume events in your CAP application can be found in this tutorial.

Once the CAP application is set up, it will automatically receive information whenever an invoice is created in the system.


Created invoice in SAP system


Created invoice in CAP Application

To register an invoice in KSeF, we need various details such as invoice data and customer information. Unfortunately, SAP's standard business events are merely notification events—they simply alert us when an invoice is created. To retrieve additional information, we must request it using the OData API.

Billing Documents and Business Partner API enabling

To consume OData services from your CAP application, follow these steps:

  • Activate OData Service in S/4HANA system
  • Create Technical User with appropriate roles
  • Configure connection between your system and SAP BTP account
  • Add Service to your CAP application 

You can find the detailed guide here.

Once the necessary OData services are enabled, you can gather all required information and send your invoice via the KSeF API.


The invoice is sent to KSeF


The invoice is successfully registered in KSeF


The invoice is available in the KSeF app

This is an practical example of how to easily integrate your S/4HANA system with KSeF using SAP Event Mesh and the Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). SAP Event Mesh enables smooth communication between systems through events, while CAP efficiently manages and processes those events. This setup automates the flow of invoice data and provides a flexible and scalable solution that can adapt to future needs.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 29th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/29/sap-developer-news-august-29th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

  • Release Notes Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

SAP TechEd Registration

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

  • New CodeJam in the topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • CodeJam event in Warsaw, Poland: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ec-p/13809704#M572

ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

  • ABAP Developer Challenge: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-abap-core-data-services/ba-p/13778288
  • Devtoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

2:15 SAP TechEd Registration

3:08 New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

4:04 ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

Transcript

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here, and yes it is time for yet again another release of the BTP ABAP environment, release 2408. And once again, this release is jam packed with new features for ABAP developers. Just to name a few, ABAP Developer Tools now supports a read-only fallback editor for development objects that do not have a native editor, for example BSPs, and also ADT now supports displaying warning messages coming from the HANA database while debugging nested AMDPs. CDS now supports external databases via ABAP CDS and ABAP SQL using CDS external entities. We also have support for logical external schemas for outbound SQL access with external entities. There has been updates to the ABAP repository object wizard, which now includes generating ABAP CDS provider models. There is also new support for creating CDS aspects to store and reuse field definitions and calculations. and we also have support for a few new annotations as well.

There are a bunch of new features around UI services in Fiori Launchpad, like support for adding a custom logo to the Launchpad and other customizing of the browser window. A lot of new features around integration topics like direct push-based integration over RFC, new enhancements around the advanced event mesh, and creating and assigning API packages.

Now I've only touched on a few of the new features to hit release 24.08. So, if you want the full story and all of the details, be sure to check out the 24.08 release blog post today. See you next time.

[Josh] It's time for SAP TechEd. Every year, TechEd is our time to get together, share ideas, learn, and have a lot of fun. Last year's TechEd was awesome, and good news, registration for TechEd 2024 is officially open. We've got an incredible TechEd lineup. First off, two full days of keynote analysis, code demos over 80 live sessions, three channels of programming, and live Q&A. That's right, they're still letting me talk live on a microphone. Then, and I am so excited about this, join us on tour as we make our way around the world with in-person stops on three continents throughout 2024 and 2025. We can't wait to connect in person for deep dive sessions, hands-on labs, and continue learning together. And trust me, this is a TechEd you will not wanna miss. See you online in October.

[Nora] Hey there, we have a new Generative AI CodeJam on the list. The first two events are already scheduled. One of them will be on the 20th of September in Warsaw, Poland at the KMD office and the other one will be right after Virtual TechEd in Munich. During the CodeJam you will learn how to access different large range models via Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. We will use the Python SDK of Generative AI Hub and we will also use the SAP AI Launchpad to access the models. You will also learn how to implement a retrieval augmented generation use case using the HANA Vector Engine and we will have a look at different chunking techniques, how you can chunk your documents right and then we will also have a look at a multi modal model where you will learn how to work with images and text. So, make sure to also check out all the other great code gems on the list, find an event near you or host your own event. See you there! Hello, SAP developers.

[Shilpa] I hope you are enjoying the August Developer Challenge on ABAP Core Data Services. The last task in this four-week challenge was out yesterday. We have seen huge response over 550 plus submissions throughout the challenge and thanks to each one of you for taking time and as well as involving in some good discussions on best practices. For those of you who have not yet taken part in the challenge, you have one more week left until we close it. In this four-week task, you will learn about the basics of ABAP series, annotations, associations, criticality and object page. While this challenge is coming to an end, we have another exciting event kicking off in September and that is Devtoberfest. We are back again with lots of interesting sessions which ranges from September 23rd until October 25th. Like last year, we have dedicated topics each day ranging from ABAP, CAP, SAP Build, Build Code, integration, machine learning, AI, and front-end. Most of the sessions are already scheduled in the community event page. Check it out and happy learning!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in August
entry.content:
Vacation period ⛱️ comes to an end, and we delivered a strong release in August. There are also lots of excellent new blogs in the community and top features on YouTube for below.

 

My top features in August

 

Compatibility Contracts

To encourage confidence in the stability of your views and to guarantee backward compatibility when they are updated, you can now set their Release State to Released. Once a view is released, it must continue to provide the same output columns until it is replaced by a successor, at which point it can be deprecated and, eventually, decommissioned.
The release states are an optional feature. By default, all views are set to Not Released and there is no obligation to release any view.
More information.

 

Workload Management

You can now set space priorities and statement limits for all the spaces in the new Workload Management page under System and Configuration. In the space details pages, you can view the values set for the space priority and statement limits, but you can no longer edit them there. 
Similarly, on the CLI we have deprecated some parameters for the space definition, and they will be removed in future releases. At the same time new ones are introduced and recommended to be used going forward. 
More information.

 

Change Space Upon Import

When working with content packages previously, you were restricted to import them into spaces with the same technical name as the spaces listed in the package. Now you can choose which spaces to import into by selecting a Space Mapping for each space listed in your package, as long as any shared objects can be resolved in the same package.

This helps you to

  • import content on the same tenant into different spaces
  • import a package more than once in the same tenant thus creating copies in different spaces
  • support for intra-tenant transport i.e., space to space transport using the Content Network

More information.

 

 

Support Content on SAP Help

@Ali_Chalhoub already started a few blogs highlighting the new Support Content pages on SAP help for SAP Datasphere. These pages cover troubleshooting and analysis guides, how-to guides, technical details, and more. This is just the beginning, and they will be extended over time.

These blog highlight some topics covered on the pages, and there is already much more:

  • Troubleshooting of Performance Issues Between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere
  • Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
  • Understanding and Documenting the Communication Flow in Remote table, Replication and Dataflow

 

My top blogs in August

@GaetanSaulnier announces the general availability of the technical integration between SAP Datasphere and the Collibra Data Intelligence Platform in his blog and shares a lot of details
➡️ SAP Datasphere and Collibra: End-to-End Data Catalog Integration

@FlorianN and @GregoryRohloff added another blog and video each to the already existing series about the data marketplace:
➡️ SAP Datasphere data marketplace - behind the scenes - new video for marketplace video series
➡️ E2E Use Case: Realizing a Data Product Architecture with SAP IT

@SandyTran started a great blog and video series covering native features in SAP Datasphere which people with strong SAP BW skills will certainly love in her blog
➡️ SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started.

 

Blogs for our Japanese Community

  • SAP Datasphereの翻訳機能による多言語設定の方法
  • SAP Datasphere ビジネスコンテンツ概要とインストール手順
  • ビジネスコンテンツ「 Finance Foundation for SAP S/4HANA and S/4HANA Cloud」を実装してみよう！
  • SAP Datasphere ビジネスコンテンツ概要とインストール手順
  • SAP Integration Suiteを使ってSAP Datasphereからデータを抽出する方法 OData編

 

More blogs to check out 👇

  • Exploring Data Access Control: Ensuring Consistency from SAC to Datasphere and Back - Blog 2
  • E2E Use Case: Realizing a Data Product Architecture with SAP IT
  • SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls Overview
  • Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) : POLICY COMPLIANCE SAC Story 
  • Datasphere to Power BI via an ODBC Connection
  • SAP Datasphere and Collibra: End-to-End Data Catalog Integration
  • SAC Report Broadcasting - Blog 1 - Imported Data
  • Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) : CREDIT CARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT SAC Story
  • Data Templates for DDS: Expense Administration SAC Story
  • SAP Datasphere data marketplace - behind the scenes - new video for marketplace video series
  • Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
  • Troubleshooting of Performance Issues Between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere
  • Understanding and Documenting the Communication Flow in Remote table, Replication and Dataflow
  • Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Expense
  • Importing Entities with Semantic of CDS views from S/4HANA On-Premise in SAP Datasphere
  • An Introduction in Automated Machine Learning with FLAML
  • Modernize your BW Landscape: SAP BW/4HANA and SAP Datasphere with SAP BW Bridge
  • SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started
  • Geo modeling in Datasphere with SAC Geo map
  • Data is the fuel for AI - Modernizing your BW landscape is preparing a data ready enterprise
  • Connect SAP Sales Cloud V2 to SAP Datasphere
  • New Feature - Ability to Disable Welcome Emails
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Lifecycle Management

 

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting and the FAQ for SAP Datasphere.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.
